title: oxalis ononifolia
intro: Oxalis ononifolia, also known as wood sorrel or creeping oxalis, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the oxalis genus. It is native to North America and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic and to reduce inflammation. The leaves and stems of the Oxalis ononifolia plant contain oxalic acid, which has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure and improving digestion. Oxalis ononifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
